concept: Poster coast where pop geometry, masked sun, and marbled tide become one pilgrimage.
scene: A mid-century travel-poster vista shows a surreal coastal plateau at dusk, unified by warm faded lithograph inks: in the foreground, a promenade of cream stone follows the edge of a moon-shaped tidal pool whose water curls in marbled black, grey, and pearl patterns, dotted with a few floating rock-islets. Along the promenade stand a rhythmic row of stylised profile statues carved as zigzag black-and-cream silhouettes edged in coral, facing the horizon like wayfinding markers. To one side, a crouching adult traveller in a broad hat appears as a simplified black silhouette stepping down toward the pool, while behind them rise tiled terraces patterned with red-and-violet checker geometry and repeating oval motifs, suggesting a modernist hillside town. In the middle ground, a grove of tall dark trees shelters draped adult figures and a sleek spotted cat rendered as elegant poster shapes, their dramatic Renaissance motion transformed into a festival scene rather than a tableau; a sinuous serpent becomes a decorative ribbon winding around a path. At the center of the bay, a bright yellow sun-emblem with jagged rays and a blue mask-like face hangs low over the water like the region’s heraldic sunset. In the background, a narrow lighthouse or sail-tower is striped in cream and red, echoing the vertical ribbon from the dark cover, and the cliffs are punctuated with mural-like facades where partial adult faces appear through violet painted blocks, as if the town itself carries weathered devotional frescoes. A vast sky of cream fading to plum ties everything together with long stylised light and a clear horizon line.
prompt: Create one mid-century travel poster illustration - simplified geometry, stylised light, smooth gradient skies, flat confident shapes, and absolutely no lettering. The image is a 16:9 music blog header. Weave in recognisable elements taken from the 8 uploaded album covers: source 1: jagged yellow sunburst with blue wide-eyed face; source 2: red-and-purple checker grid with repeating oval motifs; source 3: slender vertical red-and-cream ribbon form; source 4: marching side-profile silhouettes; source 5: dramatic pastoral figures with spotted cat and winding serpent; source 6: crouching hatted silhouette before a circular disc; source 7: marbled moon-like pool with floating droplets; source 8: adult faces obscured by rough purple painted blocks. Transform the subjects, figures, creatures, objects, and symbols depicted in the uploaded covers so they stay recognisable, and place them all inside one cohesive world with a single shared setting and consistent light. Stylise each album motif into the poster world - as landmarks, vehicles, figures, flora, or emblems drawn in the same simplified poster language. Composition: One heroic vista with a strong horizon, a big sky, and clear foreground, middle-ground, and background bands. Colour direction: cream, teal, coral, ochre, faded red - warm faded lithograph colours - cream, teal, coral, ochre - tuned to the sleeves. Mood: mysterious, ecstatic, nocturnal, and adventurous. Keep the scene safe and tasteful: feature only adults (no children, infants, or minors), and show no gore, wounds, body horror, medical or anatomical imagery, blank or milky eyes, nudity, or sexual content. Reinterpret any such source motif abstractly, and suggest rather than exactly replicate the likeness of any identifiable real person. Do not include any text, letters, words, numbers, captions, titles, logos, watermarks, or signage anywhere in the image. Avoid: readable text, letters, words, numbers, captions, titles, logos, watermarks, signage, grid layout, contact sheet, evenly tiled squares, raw album-cover thumbnails, collage of separate panels, photorealistic photograph, DSLR photo, 3D render, CGI, octane render, glossy 3D rendering, camera depth of field, lens bokeh, realistic skin texture, a posed group of people arranged for the camera, objects displayed on tables, trays, or plinths, one giant central sculpture as the centrepiece of the scene. Bold, confident, gallery-quality print design with a strong sense of place.
